evidence: "I was excited when this was announced as this was one of my favorite books growing up..."
"A solid movie. Good action, good drama."
"It's a very linear story which already is very cinematic and would translate well from page to screen."
"Most others had fallen flat and provided only casual entertainment."
"I got an hour into the movie before becoming so bored that I had to give up."
"I did enjoy the visual depiction of the tesseract."
"I have great memories of growing up watching reruns of 'Gomer Pyle' with my father."
"I wasn't necessarily pleased with all of them, but given the structure of the books, it was understandable that some work had to be done."
"I did enjoy the visual depiction of the tesseract."
"The music... was completely out of place in the 1920s jazz-age."
"It's just another movie on Russell Crowe's filmography."

summary: The user shows a preference for sci-fi and drama genres, but has a low emotional engagement with the content. They value production quality highly and prefer straightforward narratives, often finding slow pacing unappealing. Brand loyalty is low, indicating a more general appreciation for various films rather than specific creators.
